prompt: Start the pipeline. max_parallel=2, mode=waves, worker_model=auto. Save the dispatch summary to dispatch_summary.md at the sandbox root and STOP at the human-confirmation gate.
fixture: Two ready epics that both modify src/services/auth/middleware/__init__.py — they interfere and must be serialized.

assertions.text: dispatch_summary.md exists at sandbox root and is non-empty
Summary references both seeded epic IDs (epic_2fa and epic_ratelimit)
Summary includes an interference / conflict assessment between the two epics
Summary correctly classifies the epics as INTERFERING (shared file detected)
Summary mentions the shared file path src/services/auth/middleware/__init__.py (or the directory)
Summary states the epics will be serialized — not parallelized
First batch lists exactly 1 task from a single epic (serialized despite max_parallel=2)
Summary references required config: max_parallel=2, mode=waves, worker_model=auto
Summary asks the human for confirmation (proceed / confirm / abort)
No bd task was newly claimed — no implementers were dispatched
No new git commits beyond the seed commit
